name: Python Data Types Assembly
description: Assemble expressions involving strings, numbers, booleans, and mutability basics.

questions.question: Assemble code to convert string s to integer.
questions.explanation: Use int(s) to parse integer text.

questions.question: Assemble code to convert x to string.
Assemble code to lowercase variable text.
Assemble code to check if n is even.
questions.explanation: Even numbers have remainder 0 when divided by 2.

questions.question: Assemble code to round value x to two decimals.
Assemble condition checking if name starts with 'A'.
Assemble code to test if character ch is a digit.
questions.explanation: isdigit returns True when all chars are digits.

questions.question: Assemble boolean negation of variable done.
questions.explanation: Python uses not for boolean negation.

questions.question: Assemble code to concatenate first and last name with a space.
questions.explanation: String concatenation with + builds combined name.

questions.question: Assemble code to create a shallow copy of list nums.
questions.explanation: Both slicing and copy() produce a shallow list copy.

questions.question: Assemble code to check if object x is None.
questions.explanation: is None is the canonical None check.

questions.question: Assemble code to compute absolute value of n.
Assemble code to join list parts with '-' separator.
Assemble code to parse floating number from text s.
Assemble condition to check if value x is truthy and y is truthy.
questions.explanation: and requires both operands to be truthy.
